behaviors.description: Log entries with the same trace ID are grouped together regardless of which microservice emitted them.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is grouping logic itself (keying on trace_id, not service). This can be reproduced with an in-memory set of parsed log objects from multiple services; no real OS/network/subprocess or AI output is needed. Higher level rejected: Integration is not required because behavior text does not require parser/repo/report handoff, only grouping semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Call the correlator/grouping function with mixed-service log entries sharing trace IDs and assert all same-trace entries land in one group independent of service name.

behaviors.description: The timeline view orders entries by timestamp, correctly handling clock skew of up to 500ms between services.
behaviors.justification: This is ordering/skew-correction algorithm behavior on local data. The failure is in sort/adjustment logic, not real infrastructure. Higher levels rejected: System is disqualified because no real HTTP/OS/subprocess requirement is stated; Integration is unnecessary unless module handoff is part of behavior text (it is not).
behaviors.test_description: Feed a timeline-ordering function synthetic entries from two services with known +/-500ms skew and assert resulting order matches causal expected order after skew handling.

behaviors.description: A latency spike (p99 above 3x the baseline) is detected and flagged in the anomaly report.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly spans at least two local modules: percentile/baseline detector and anomaly report output. Real failure can occur at module boundary (detected but not reported, or threshold mismatch between modules). Integration brake: no real OS/network/subprocess needed; no AI/ML output dependency; no multi-agent chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run detector + report builder together on in-memory latency windows where p99 exceeds 3x baseline; assert anomaly record appears in final report with correct metric/threshold context.

behaviors.description: Malformed JSON log entries are skipped without crashing the pipeline, and the count of skipped entries is reported.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is parser-to-pipeline error propagation plus counter/reporting state. Unit tests of parser alone can miss pipeline crash or miscount. Integration brake: no required real OS/network/subprocess in behavior text; no AI dependence; no multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Execute ingest->parse->aggregate pipeline in-process with a mixed stream of valid and malformed JSON lines; assert pipeline completes, valid entries are processed, and skipped_count equals malformed input count.

behaviors.description: The tool handles a burst of 10,000 log entries per second without dropping entries or falling behind.
behaviors.justification: This is a throughput/backpressure behavior that depends on real runtime scheduling, buffering, and timing under load. In-process mocks can hide drops/lag caused by actual OS/process behavior. Higher levels rejected: Agentic/Workflow disqualified because behavior has no AI/LLM steps.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real executable/service with a load generator at 10k entries/sec for a sustained interval; measure ingest count vs produced timeline/anomaly output and processing lag to verify zero drops and bounded backlog growth.
